hero_description: Bitweb Core is an open-source cryptocurrency implementing the Argon2id proof-of-work algorithm — ASIC-resistant, memory-hard, and built for fair mining.
home_feat1_body: Memory-hard proof-of-work resistant to ASIC mining. Fair distribution through CPU and GPU accessibility.
home_feat2_body: Fully open-source under the MIT license. Auditable, forkable, community-driven development on GitHub.
home_feat3_body: Deterministic builds via GNU Guix. Every binary is independently verifiable. GPG-signed release attestations.
news_empty: No posts yet. Check back soon.
download_subtitle: Choose your platform and verify your download.
download_error: Failed to load releases. Please visit
verify_intro: Always verify the SHA256 checksum and GPG signature before running any binary.
verify_guix_body: Bitweb Core is built reproducibly using GNU Guix. Builder attestations and keys are published at
wallets_web_lead: Browser-based wallet — no installation required. Your keys stay on your device.
wallets_web_warning: This is a non-custodial web wallet. Never share your seed phrase or private keys with anyone.
mining_lead: Bitweb uses the Argon2id proof-of-work algorithm — mineable with CPU and GPU.
mining_solo_lead: Mine directly to your own full node. You keep 100% of block rewards.
mining_step2_desc: Windows and Linux builds — choose the binary that matches your CPU:
mining_pools_lead: Pool mining gives you steady payouts. Connect using stratum settings shown on each pool's dashboard.
resources_lead: Tools, explorers, exchanges, and community links for Bitweb.
dev_contribute_body: Bitweb Core is an open contributor project — anyone is welcome to contribute via peer review, testing, and patches. There is no privileged developer class; contributors earn trust through merit. Repository maintainers are responsible for merging pull requests and the release cycle.
dev_rpc_intro: Complete JSON-RPC API reference for bitweb-cli and the RPC interface.
footer_rights: Open-source software, released under the MIT License.
spec_subtitle: Full technical parameters of the Bitweb (BTE) network
spec_pow_note: Block identifiers are hashed with SHA256d; Argon2id serves as the PoW challenge only — the same separation used in Litecoin (Scrypt PoW / SHA256d chain).
spec_addr_note: Version bytes shown as decimal and hex. All address types are 34 characters.
vest_subtitle: BTE is a full network relaunch with a clean genesis, fair mining, and built-in vesting protection for distributed coins.
vest_launch_body: BTE is a full relaunch of a previous chain. Holders of the old network received new BTE at a 100:1 denomination ratio — one new BTE per 100 old coins. To maintain mathematical and aesthetic integrity, the first 60,000 blocks were mined the standard way: a flat 50 BTE reward per block, no inflated coinbase outputs, no shortcuts — pure mining as a foundation for fair distribution.
vest_protect_body: To prevent sudden sell pressure from the distributed supply, all coinbase outputs mined during the extended period (blocks 60,000–69,300) carry an extended maturity lock. The lock is designed so that every coin from this period becomes spendable at the same moment — block 69,400 — giving the network time to find a natural price equilibrium before unlocked coins can move.
vest_block1_desc: Extended coinbase maturity begins. All coins from this window unlock at block 69,400.
vest_block2_desc: Last block subject to extended lock. The window spans 9,300 blocks ≈ 32 days.
vest_block3_desc: All extended-period coinbases mature simultaneously. Standard 100-block maturity (~8 h) resumes.
vest_miner_body: Your blocks mine and pay out normally — standard 50 BTE reward at every height. If your block falls inside the extended window (60,000–69,300), your coinbase will mature later than usual, but all such coins unlock together at block 69,400, roughly 32 days from the window start. Outside this range the standard 100-block maturity (~8 hours) applies as always.
vest_pool_body: A pool earning 50 BTE per block will accumulate over 1,600 unspent coinbase outputs by the time it holds 80,000 BTE. Creating a payout transaction from that many inputs can exceed the transaction size limit and return an error. Before each scheduled payout run, consolidate your wallet: send batches of no more than 50,000 BTE to your own address in separate transactions. Perform consolidation after block 69,400 to avoid extended-maturity errors.
vest_pool_badge: Before large payouts: consolidate in batches of ≤ 50,000 BTE
dev_lead: Contribute to Bitweb Core or explore the RPC API.
about_intro: Bitweb Core is an open-source project that maintains and releases the Bitweb (BTE) client software. Based on Bitcoin Core, it provides both a full-node for fully validating the blockchain and a built-in wallet. Anyone is welcome to contribute.
about_team_note: This list will be expanded. We hope Bitweb Core will grow a large open-source developer community with many contributors to the codebase.
about_maintainers_body: Project maintainers have commit access and are responsible for merging patches from contributors. They perform a janitorial role — merging patches the team agrees should be merged — and act as a final check to ensure patches are safe and in line with project goals. The maintainers' role is by agreement of project contributors.
about_contributors_body: Everyone is free to propose code changes and to test, review, and comment on open Pull Requests. Anyone who contributes code, review, tests, translations, or documentation to Bitweb Core is considered a contributor. The release notes for each Bitweb Core release contain a credits section recognizing all who contributed over the previous release cycle.
about_bugs_body: Use the GitHub issue tracker to report bugs in the software.
about_community_body: Get help and discuss Bitweb with the community:
about_website_bugs_body: Found a problem on this website? Use the website issue tracker.
about_security_versions_body: Bitweb Core maintains security updates for the two most recent major release series. See the lifecycle schedule for currently supported versions:
about_security_report_body: To report a security issue, send an email to security@bitwebcore.net — not for general support. Please follow responsible disclosure practices.
lifecycle_lead: Bitweb Core maintains security updates for the two most recent major release series. Older series reach End-of-Life (EOL) when a newer series supersedes them.
dev_build_intro: Build guides are maintained in the repository for all supported platforms.
brand_lead: Official Bitweb Core logos for use in media and documentation.
brand_usage_body: Use these assets to refer to Bitweb Core in articles, integrations, and applications. Do not alter the logo colors or proportions. Do not use the logo to imply endorsement.
brand_svg_note: SVG is resolution-independent and preferred for all uses.
dev_wp_intro: Technical specification covering Argon2id proof-of-work, LWMA-3 difficulty, monetary policy, and the genesis airdrop.
